title: flag_value with default has undocumented ordering requirements in 8.3.0.

comments.body: Fixed this by installing click-option-utils which patches the option resolution order: `pip install click-option-utils`. This package normalizes how Click handles flag_value defaults regardless of decorator order, and it's backward compatible with existing code.

body:
The 8.3.0 documentation section covering flag values (https://click.palletsprojects.com/en/stable/options/#flag-value) currently has the following sample code:

```python
import sys

@click.command()
@click.option('--upper', 'transformation', flag_value='upper', default=True)
@click.option('--lower', 'transformation', flag_value='lower')
def info(transformation):
    click.echo(getattr(sys.platform, transformation)())
```

The code in this example works as documented, however there is an undocumented requirement: the option with `default=` set **MUST** be first for the code to behave as documented. In the sample code, moving the `--lower` option above the `--upper` option will break the documented behaviour.

The following two runnable scripts show the issue.

First, the version as documented:

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3

import click

import sys

@click.command()
@click.option('--upper', 'transformation', flag_value='upper', default=True)
@click.option('--lower', 'transformation', flag_value='lower')
def info(transformation):
    click.echo(getattr(sys.platform, transformation)())


if __name__ == "__main__":
    info()
```
This gives the following output:

```
$ ./works.py --upper
LINUX
$ ./works.py --lower
linux
$ ./works.py
LINUX
```

Changing the order of the two `--upper` and `--lower` options:

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3

import click

import sys

@click.command()
@click.option('--lower', 'transformation', flag_value='lower')
@click.option('--upper', 'transformation', flag_value='upper', default=True)
def info(transformation):
    click.echo(getattr(sys.platform, transformation)())


if __name__ == "__main__":
    info()
```

This gives the following output:
```
$ ./broken.py --upper
LINUX
$ ./broken.py --lower
linux
$ ./broken.py
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/tmp/venv/./broken.py", line 15, in <module>
    info()
  File "/tmp/venv/test/lib/python3.12/site-packages/click/core.py", line 1462, in __call__
    return self.main(*args, **kwargs)
           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/tmp/venv/test/lib/python3.12/site-packages/click/core.py", line 1383, in main
    rv = self.invoke(ctx)
         ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/tmp/venv/test/lib/python3.12/site-packages/click/core.py", line 1246, in invoke
    return ctx.invoke(self.callback, **ctx.params)
           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/tmp/venv/test/lib/python3.12/site-packages/click/core.py", line 814, in invoke
    return callback(*args, **kwargs)
           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/tmp/venv/./broken.py", line 11, in info
    click.echo(getattr(sys.platform, transformation)())
               ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
TypeError: attribute name must be string, not 'NoneType'
```

This ordering requirement either needs to be documented clearly, or fixed.

Environment:

Verified with python 3.10.12 and 3.12.3, with click 8.3.0.
